title: Myriopteris lendigera

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Myriopteris lendigera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the maidenhair fern is often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
taxonomy: Myriopteris lendigera is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The genus Myriopteris is a group of small to medium-sized ferns that are often found growing in rocky or sandy areas.
